cards.back: Gross Profit / Revenue × 100%. It measures the percentage of revenue remaining after deducting cost of goods sold.
Net Profit / Revenue × 100%. It measures the percentage of revenue remaining after all expenses. Shows overall profitability.
Net Profit / Average Total Assets × 100%. It measures how efficiently assets generate profit.
Net Profit / Average Shareholders' Equity × 100%. It measures the return earned on shareholders' investment.
Current Assets / Current Liabilities. Measures ability to pay short-term obligations. Ideal ratio is 2:1 or higher.
(Current Assets - Inventory) / Current Liabilities. More conservative than current ratio; ideal is 1:1 or higher.
Current Assets - Current Liabilities. Shows the absolute dollar amount of short-term liquidity available for operations.
Debt / (Debt + Equity) × 100%. Measures financial risk and leverage. High gearing (>50%) indicates heavy reliance on debt.

cards.front: What is the interest coverage ratio?
cards.back: Operating Profit / Interest Expense. Shows ability to service debt. Higher is better; ideal is 2.5× or more.
Revenue / Average Total Assets. Measures how efficiently assets generate revenue. Higher indicates better asset utilisation.
Net Profit / Number of Shares Outstanding. Shows profit attributable to each share. Higher EPS is more attractive to investors.
Comparing the same line items across different time periods to identify trends and percentage changes year-over-year.
